_meta.title: Financial Shenanigans Forensic Engine — Technical Specification Ledger
_meta.authors: Howard M. Schilit, Jeremy Perler, Yoni Engelhart
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source-of-truth contract ledger for compute_schilit_forensic_score() in forensic_engine.py. All 4 checker thresholds, point deductions, and pass/fail gate are codified here. The test harness (tests/test_schilit_contract.py) parses this file and asserts that the live code matches every value.
_meta.book_reading_status: COMPLETE — full 337-page PDF extracted and read 2026-05-24. Chapters 1-19 read including all new Part 5 (Acquisition Accounting Shenanigans, Ch.15-17). Implementation updated based on direct book content.
_meta.design_rationale: Schilit 4th Ed. organises manipulation into 4 categories: Earnings Manipulation Shenanigans (EMS 1-7, Ch.3-9), Cash Flow Shenanigans (CFS 1-3, Ch.10-12), Key Metrics Shenanigans (KMS 1-2, Ch.13-14), and Acquisition Accounting Shenanigans (AA 1-3, Ch.15-17 — NEW in 4th Ed.). The 4 checkers map to EMS+CFS+KMS using pre-computed data_engine.py signals. AA shenanigans cannot be implemented without goodwill, acquisition spend, and non-GAAP vs. GAAP comparison data not present in the Screener.in CSV export (see 'aa_shenanigans_implementation_gap' section). Scoring: 100 - (15 x checkers_fired). Pass threshold 70 = at most 2 of 4 checkers may fire. All logic is fully vectorized — zero apply(), zero iterrows().

initial_score.rationale: All stocks start forensically clean. Penalties subtract from 100 for each manipulation pattern detected.

per_checker_deduction.rationale: Each of the 4 independent Schilit checker categories deducts 15 points when fired. Four checkers = max 60 point deduction, minimum score 40. Deduction is symmetrical — no checker outweighs another, reflecting Schilit's framework parity across EMS/CFS/KMS.

pass_threshold.rationale: Score >= 70 = at most 2 of 4 checkers fire = schilit_pass = 1. Score < 70 = 3 or 4 checkers fire = schilit_pass = 0. A stock with 3+ independent manipulation signals across EMS/CFS/KMS is a clear forensic rejection.

min_possible_score.rationale: All 4 checkers fire: 100 - 4*15 = 40. Score is clipped to [0, 100].

checker_1_ems._shenanigans: EMS #1 (Revenue too early), EMS #3 (One-time boosting), EMS #4 (Expense capitalisation), EMS #7 (Big bath charges)
checker_1_ems._chapter_refs: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.3 (EMS#1: DSO/channel stuffing), Ch.5 (EMS#3: Intel/Marvell option-income, Sunbeam insurance settlement), Ch.6 (EMS#4: AOL marketing cost capitalisation), Ch.9 (EMS#7: WorldCom $3.8B capex reclassification). India Forensic Edition Ch.2, Ch.8 (15-metric dashboard), Ch.10 (Beneish M-Score TATA coefficient)
checker_1_ems._india_cases: Satyam (EMS#2+#5), Manpasand (EMS#1 — receivables growing 3× revenue), Vakrangee (EMS#2+KMS#1)
checker_1_ems._book_cases: Computer Associates (DSO 247d; EMS#1 Ch.3), AOL (marketing costs capitalised EMS#4 Ch.6+9), Intel (option income boosting EPS Ch.5), Marvell (tax-rate engineering Ch.5), Sunbeam (insurance settlement as recurring income Ch.5), WorldCom ($3.8B capex reclassification EMS#7 Ch.9)
checker_1_ems.description: Fires if ANY of 5 signals confirm earnings manipulation: (1) accruals ratio above 5% of total assets — the Beneish M-Score TATA variable with the highest fraud coefficient (4.679); (2) inventory growing more than 20pp faster than revenue — channel stuffing / demand weakness concealment; (3) OPM more than 30% off 5-year median — volatile margins indicate a commodity price-taker or margin manipulation; (4) NPM exceeds OPM by more than 5pp — significant non-operating income boosting reported PAT (EMS#3 one-time gains); (5) asset turnover declining 0.10+ units while OPM simultaneously improving 2pp+ — the AOL expense capitalisation fingerprint (EMS#4).

signal_1_accruals.schilit_chapter: Chapter 2 EMS#4, Chapter 5 Stage-2 Check-5, Chapter 10 Beneish TATA
signal_1_accruals.rationale: Beneish TATA coefficient = 4.679 — the single strongest predictor of earnings manipulation in the 8-variable M-Score. accruals_ratio = (PAT - OCF) / Avg_Total_Assets > 5% means reported earnings systematically exceed cash generation. Threshold 5% matches both Beneish original paper and India Forensic handbook Ch.5.

signal_2_inv_rev_gap.schilit_chapter: Chapter 2 EMS#1, Chapter 8 metric #3 (DIO rising >20 days/year)
signal_2_inv_rev_gap.rationale: Inventory growing more than 20pp faster than revenue signals unsold product accumulation (channel stuffing), demand weakness, or obsolescence. Book threshold: DIO rising >20 days/year is a Level 1 red flag (Ch.8 dashboard). Using percentage-point gap rather than DIO directly because data_engine pre-computes inv_growth vs rev_gr_yoy.

signal_3_opm_stability.schilit_chapter: Chapter 2 EMS#3 (unsustainable income), Chapter 7 (commodity sector margins)
signal_3_opm_stability.rationale: OPM deviating >30% from 5-year median indicates either one-time income boosting (EMS#3) or a commodity price-taker with no pricing power. Consistent with rf_opm_volatile in compute_red_flags() which uses the same 30% threshold. Linked to rf_opm_volatile — both use identical 30% deviation threshold.

signal_4_other_income_inflate.precomputed_in: data_engine.py — npm (annual NPM %), opm (annual OPM %); both from Screener.in Ratio CSV
signal_4_other_income_inflate.schilit_chapter: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.5 EMS#3 (Boosting Income Using One-Time or Unsustainable Activities)
signal_4_other_income_inflate.book_cases: Intel (Ch.5): option-income persistently boosted EPS above operating earnings. Marvell (Ch.5): tax-rate engineering created persistent gap between net and operating margins. Sunbeam (Ch.5): insurance settlement from warehouse fire classified as recurring operating income. Dunkin Donuts: franchise fees and investment income creating NPM >> OPM gap.
signal_4_other_income_inflate.rationale: When net profit margin exceeds operating profit margin by more than 5 percentage points, the company's bottom line is being sustained by non-operating income streams — investment gains, asset sales, one-time insurance settlements, subsidies, or related-party income — rather than core business operations. This is the quantitative fingerprint of EMS#3. The 5pp threshold is conservative enough to exclude companies with modest interest income from cash holdings, while capturing material non-operating income inflation. NaN in either column → difference is NaN → NaN > 5.0 = False in pandas (safe, does not fire on missing data).

signal_5_expense_cap_proxy.precomputed_in: data_engine.py — asset_turnover, asset_turnover_1yb (Screener.in Ratio CSV); opm, opm_1yb (annual OPM from Ratio CSV)
signal_5_expense_cap_proxy.schilit_chapter: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.6 EMS#4 (Shifting Current Expenses to a Later Period), Ch.9 EMS#7 (Big Bath / WorldCom)
signal_5_expense_cap_proxy.book_cases: AOL (Ch.6 EMS#4): subscriber acquisition costs ($385M) capitalised over 2 years → OPM improved dramatically from 2% to 19% while asset base grew and asset turnover fell. SEC charged AOL with improper expense capitalisation in 2000. WorldCom (Ch.9 EMS#7): $3.8B line costs reclassified from expenses to capital assets → EBITDA margins inflated while asset turnover ratio fell from 0.97 to 0.75 over 3 years. Both cases share the dual fingerprint: OPM rising + AT declining.
signal_5_expense_cap_proxy.rationale: Asset turnover = Revenue / Total Assets. When a company capitalises expenses (moves operating costs from P&L to balance sheet), two things happen simultaneously: (1) operating expenses fall → OPM improves; (2) total assets grow faster than revenue → asset turnover declines. This dual signal is the mechanical fingerprint of expense capitalisation. Using 0.10 unit AT decline (material: ~5-7% on a typical AT of 1.5-2.0) AND 2pp OPM improvement (above quarterly noise) ensures both conditions must be simultaneously true. NaN in any column → False (safe).

signal_6_depreciation_rate_manipulation.expression: (fixed_assets > fixed_assets_1yb * 1.05) AND (dep_rate < dep_rate_1yb * 0.80) AND NOT is_financial
signal_6_depreciation_rate_manipulation.precomputed_in: data_engine.py — dep_rate = (ebitda - ebit) / fixed_assets * 100; dep_rate_1yb = (ebitda_1yb - ebit_1yb) / fixed_assets_1yb * 100. EBIT and EBIT 1 Year Back added to INCOME_COLS mapping 2026-05-24 (were in Screener.in CSV but previously unmapped).
signal_6_depreciation_rate_manipulation.data_requirement_resolved: EBIT column added to data_engine.py INCOME_COLS on 2026-05-24. D&A = EBITDA − EBIT is the exact accounting identity. dep_rate = D&A / Fixed_Assets × 100 (%). This signal is now fully computable from existing Screener.in CSV data.
signal_6_depreciation_rate_manipulation.schilit_chapter: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.6 EMS#4 (Shifting Current Expenses to a Later Period — 'Changing Accounting Estimates'), Ch.9 EMS#7 (WorldCom Big Bath + multi-year life extension)
signal_6_depreciation_rate_manipulation.book_cases: Qwest Communications (Ch.6 EMS#4): extended cable asset depreciation lives from 14 years to 40 years. D&A fell by approximately $1 billion/year. FA was growing; dep/FA fell from ~7.1% to ~2.5% (65% fall). SEC enforcement action; $100M settlement. WorldCom (Ch.9 EMS#7): reclassified $3.8 billion of operating line costs as 'capital assets'. These were then depreciated over 10-40 years instead of being expensed immediately. FA grew; dep_rate fell sharply. Led to the largest US accounting fraud ($11B restatement) and bankruptcy. Sunbeam (Ch.5 EMS#3+EMS#4): extended useful lives of intangibles and fixed assets alongside big bath restructuring charges — reducing future D&A to inflate post-restructuring earnings.
signal_6_depreciation_rate_manipulation.rationale: When a company extends the accounting useful life of its fixed assets (e.g., from 10 years to 14 years), the annual D&A charge falls (from 10% to ~7.1% of FA per year). This directly reduces reported expenses and inflates EBIT and PAT without any operational improvement. The Schilit signal requires BOTH: (1) FA is growing — the company is actively investing, making the life-extension decision a deliberate management choice rather than a declining-base artefact; (2) dep/FA ratio fell >20% — the effective useful life extended by at least 25% (10yr → 12.5yr minimum). The combination of these two conditions creates an unfakeable dual fingerprint. Financial stocks excluded: banks have trivially small fixed assets relative to revenue; dep/FA is structurally near-zero and not a relevant signal.

checker_1_ems.logic: accruals_warning == 1 OR inv_vs_rev_gap > 20.0 OR opm_stability > 30.0 OR (npm - opm) > 5.0 OR ((asset_turnover_1yb - asset_turnover) > 0.10 AND (opm - opm_1yb) > 2.0) OR (fixed_assets > fixed_assets_1yb * 1.05 AND dep_rate < dep_rate_1yb * 0.80 AND NOT is_financial)

checker_2_cfs._shenanigans: CFS #1 (Financing->Operating), CFS #2 (Moving outflows to other sections), CFS #3 (Boosting OCF unsustainably)
checker_2_cfs._chapter_refs: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.10 (CFS#1: Peregrine fake AR sales, Delphi inventory-as-operating), Ch.11 (CFS#2: payables stretching, Home Depot DPO 23→34d=$3B boost), Ch.12 (CFS#3: Cardinal Health AR sales $800M CFFO+$971M, Tesla deposits $350M=88% of improvement, Sun settlement). India Forensic Edition Ch.3, Ch.1 (CFO/PAT never lies), Ch.8 metric #1
checker_2_cfs._india_cases: Satyam (fake cash not generating interest), DHFL (financing-into-operating reclassification)
checker_2_cfs._book_cases: Cardinal Health (sold $800M AR → CFFO jumped $971M; Ch.12), Peregrine (fake AR sales to cover bogus revenue; Ch.10), Home Depot (DPO stretched 23→34 days = $3B unsustainable boost; Ch.11), Tesla (Model 3 deposits $350M = 88% of OCF improvement; Ch.12), Sun Microsystems (one-time settlement inflating CFFO; Ch.12)
checker_2_cfs.description: Fires if ANY of 3 signals confirm cash flow distortion: (1) earnings accelerating while operating cash flow simultaneously shrinking — the classic accrual divergence; (2) 'Paper Profits' label — cfo_to_pat <= 80% means company consistently converts less than 80% of reported profit to operating cash; (3) cfo_to_ebitda < 60% — OCF converts less than 60% of EBITDA, indicating structural CFFO inflation via AR sales, DPO stretching, or deposit games (Schilit Ch.12 CFS#3).

signal_1_earnings_cash_divergence.schilit_chapter: Chapter 1 (CFO/PAT first commandment), Chapter 3 CFS #2 (unsustainable OCF)
signal_1_earnings_cash_divergence.rationale: PAT growing >15% while OCF shrinks >15% simultaneously is the textbook accrual trap signature. The threshold asymmetry (+15 / -15) ensures we only flag material divergence, not noise. Book: CFO/PAT below 0.7 for 2+ years preceded every major Indian fraud (Satyam, DHFL, Manpasand). This checker captures the dynamic (direction) rather than the static level.

signal_2_paper_profits.rationale: India Forensic handbook Ch.1: CFO/PAT below 0.7 for any single year is a Level 1 forensic alert. cash_machine_label == '📄 Paper Profits' means cfo_to_pat <= 80% (the cash_machine_score == 0 branch). This is a more conservative threshold than the book's 0.7 minimum — catches companies in the 70-80% grey zone.

signal_3_cfo_ebitda_weakness.precomputed_in: data_engine.py — cfo_to_ebitda from Screener.in 'CFO To EBITDA' ratio column; stored as percentage (e.g. 73.04 = 73%)
signal_3_cfo_ebitda_weakness.schilit_chapter: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.12 CFS#3 (Boosting Cash Flow from Operations Using Unsustainable Activities)
signal_3_cfo_ebitda_weakness.book_cases: Cardinal Health (Ch.12): sold $800M of AR to a bank → CFFO jumped $971M in one quarter; the structural OCF/EBITDA ratio was far below 60% once the one-time AR sale is excluded. Home Depot (Ch.11 CFS#2): stretched DPO from 23 to 34 days over 5 years — $3B of CFFO 'improvement' was pure payables management; structural OCF/EBITDA deteriorated once DPO plateaued. Tesla (Ch.12): customer deposits for Model 3 ($350M) = 88% of total OCF improvement — when deposits reversed, OCF/EBITDA collapsed. Sun Microsystems (Ch.12): one-time litigation settlement inflated CFFO; ex-settlement OCF/EBITDA was <55%.
signal_3_cfo_ebitda_weakness.rationale: EBITDA represents the theoretical maximum operating cash generation before working capital movements. When OCF/EBITDA falls below 60%, it signals that working capital is consuming more than 40% of EBITDA — a structural red flag indicating receivables inflation, inventory accumulation, payables that cannot be stretched further, or one-time operating cash boosts (AR sales, deposits, settlements) that are suppressing the true conversion ratio. Threshold 60.0% is conservative: rf_low_cfo_ebitda (red flags) uses 90%, the Coffee Can standard. The Schilit CFS#3 checker uses the stricter 60% floor — only the most severe cases of CFFO inflation are flagged here. Note: stored as percentage so cfo_to_ebitda=73.04 means 73%, threshold comparison is against 60.0 (not 0.60). Financial stocks excluded: for banks/NBFCs OCF and EBITDA are structurally different metrics.

checker_2_cfs.logic: (pat_gr_yoy > 15.0 AND ocf_growth < -15.0) OR cash_machine_label == '📄 Paper Profits' OR (cfo_to_ebitda < 60.0 AND NOT is_financial)

checker_3_kms_leverage._shenanigans: KMS #6 (Balance sheet metrics distortion), EMS #5 (Hidden obligations via off-BS entities)
checker_3_kms_leverage._chapter_refs: Financial-Shenanigans-India-Forensic-Edition.md Chapter 4 KMS#6, Chapter 2 EMS#5, Chapter 6 Cases 3 (IL&FS) and 8 (Amtek Auto)
checker_3_kms_leverage._india_cases: IL&FS (347 subsidiaries hiding 91,000 Cr debt), Amtek Auto (payable stretching + hidden leverage), DHFL (RPT loans)
checker_3_kms_leverage.description: Fires if ANY of 2 signals confirm balance sheet leverage manipulation: (1) total liabilities growing faster than reported debt — hidden off-balance-sheet obligations using IL&FS-style entity structures; (2) high cash AND high debt simultaneously — Malik Shenanigan 4: optically strong balance sheet masking true leverage and often indicating restricted/pledged cash.

signal_1_hidden_obligation.schilit_chapter: Chapter 2 EMS#5, Chapter 4 KMS#6, Chapter 6 Case 3 (IL&FS)
signal_1_hidden_obligation.rationale: When total liabilities grow faster than reported debt, the gap reveals hidden obligations — lease liabilities, provisions, contingent obligations, off-BS SPV debt — exactly what EMS#5 and KMS#6 address. IL&FS hid 91,000 Cr of debt this way across 347 entities.

signal_2_high_cash_high_debt.rationale: A company with large cash holdings AND high debt simultaneously is likely holding restricted/pledged cash or has subsidiaries with cross-guarantees. The cash cannot be used to repay the debt — creating an illusion of financial strength. Vijay Malik Shenanigan #4.

checker_4_kms_bloat._shenanigans: EMS #1 (Revenue too early — DSO rising/bloated), KMS #2 (Distorted Balance Sheet metrics to hide deterioration)
checker_4_kms_bloat._chapter_refs: Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.3 (EMS#1: CA DSO 247d, 20-day YoY rise detected fraud), Ch.4 (EMS#2: Hanergy DSO 500d, 57% past due), Ch.14 (KMS#2: Symbol Tech 119d→90d via AR-to-notes reclassification; UTStarcom bank notes; Sanmina AR sales lower DSO). India Forensic Edition Ch.2 EMS#1, Ch.3 CFS#4, Ch.8 metrics #2 and #3
checker_4_kms_bloat._india_cases: Manpasand (receivables growing 3× faster than revenue — channel stuffing), Gitanjali (inventory overvaluation), Vakrangee (DSO exploded from 30d→180d)
checker_4_kms_bloat._book_cases: Computer Associates (DSO 247 days; EMS#1 Ch.3), Hanergy Solar (DSO 500 days; EMS#2 Ch.4), Symbol Technologies (DSO 94→119→90 via notes conversion Ch.14), UTStarcom (AR reclassified as bank notes in cash section Ch.14), Syntax-Brillian (120-day terms to related-party SCHOT Ch.4), Trex Company (extended payment terms → DSO spike Ch.3)
checker_4_kms_bloat.description: Fires if DSO expanded >15 days over 3 years OR inventory days grew >20 days YoY OR ABSOLUTE DSO > 90 days — for non-financial and non-high-DSO-sector stocks only. IT/Software, IT-Hardware, Healthcare, and Pharmaceuticals sectors are excluded because 60-90 day billing cycles are structurally normal for them. Financial stocks are excluded via is_financial (no inventory/receivables dynamics). Signal 3 (absolute DSO > 90d) added after full book reading: Schilit's Ch.3 shows CA at 247d and Ch.4 shows Hanergy at 500d — both clearly beyond 90d threshold for non-IT sectors.

sector_exclusions.rationale: These 4 sector groups contain all IT, Pharma, and Healthcare sub-industries (81 sector master list). Long billing cycles (60-90 days) are contractual and normal — flagging them as manipulation is a false positive. Consistent with rf_high_receivables sector-aware threshold in compute_red_flags(). Book confirms: IT companies like TCS/Infosys have 60-90 day structural DSO.

financial_exclusion.rationale: Banks and NBFCs have no inventory and trade receivables are loans. DSO and inventory metrics are structurally meaningless and always trigger false flags.

signal_1_dso_delta_3y.schilit_chapter: Schilit Ch.3 EMS#1 (CA: 20-day YoY rise triggered fraud detection; book quote: 'An increase in DSO can often be an indicator that more products were shipped late in a quarter than usual'), India Forensic Ch.8 metric #2
signal_1_dso_delta_3y.rationale: Book exact quote Ch.3: 'An increase in DSO can often be an indicator that more products were shipped late in a quarter than usual.' The 15-day threshold over 3 years means ~5 days/year expansion — consistent with the book's 20-day YoY trigger. Using 3-year delta prevents seasonal noise. Threshold 15.0 matches the India Forensic handbook figure.

signal_2_inventory_days_change.schilit_chapter: Schilit Ch.3 EMS#1 (inventory growing faster than revenue = channel stuffing before revenue falters), India Forensic Ch.8 metric #3
signal_2_inventory_days_change.rationale: India Forensic handbook Ch.8 dashboard: 'Days Inventory trend: Red Flag = Rising >20 days/year'. Inventory accumulating faster than it sells signals demand slowdown or channel stuffing before write-downs hit the P&L. Threshold 20.0 matches the exact book figure.

signal_3_absolute_dso.schilit_chapter: Schilit Ch.3 EMS#1 (Computer Associates DSO reached 247 days), Ch.4 EMS#2 (Hanergy Solar DSO reached 500 days — '57% of trade receivables listed as past due'), Ch.14 KMS#2 (Symbol Technologies: DSO 94→119 days before reclassification trick; UTStarcom: DSO distorted via bank notes)
signal_3_absolute_dso.rationale: ADDED v1.1 after full book reading. Schilit shows absolute DSO levels above 90 days (for non-IT/Pharma/Healthcare sectors) are clear fraud/manipulation signals regardless of trend direction. CA reached 247 days, Hanergy 500 days. For Indian manufacturing/FMCG/retail companies where 45-60 days is normal, >90 days is a firm absolute warning — even if the trend is flat (company may have already hidden the trend via AR reclassification, as Symbol did). Threshold 90.0 days is conservative enough to exclude infrastructure/B2B companies with legitimate 60-90d cycles while catching clear abuse. Sector exclusions (_HIGH_DSO_SECTORS) ensure IT/Pharma are not penalised.

checker_4_kms_bloat.logic: NOT (is_high_dso_sector OR is_financial) AND (dso_delta_3y > 15.0 OR inventory_days_change > 20.0 OR days_receivable > 90.0)

aa_shenanigans_implementation_gap._title: Acquisition Accounting Shenanigans (AA #1-3, Schilit 4th Ed. Ch.15-17)
aa_shenanigans_implementation_gap._status: NOT IMPLEMENTED — data unavailable in Screener.in CSV export
aa_shenanigans_implementation_gap._chapters: Ch.15 (AA#1: Inflating Revenue/Earnings), Ch.16 (AA#2: Inflating CFFO), Ch.17 (AA#3: Manipulating Key Metrics)

_key_cases_from_book.Tyco: 700+ acquisitions 1999-2002 ($29B). CFFO consistently positive but FCF-after-acquisitions was NEGATIVE every year. Pre-close cash management trick: slow collections + prepay vendors before close, then reverse after. (Ch.16)
_key_cases_from_book.WorldCom: Serial acquirer showing strong CFFO from acquisition accounting quirk — all capex in investing section while inherited working capital collections in operating section. (Ch.16)
_key_cases_from_book.Valeant: 4 major acquisitions with manipulated pre-close revenue (Salix Q4 2014: $13M revenue vs normal $300M+/quarter). Post-close revenue recognition policy changes (Medicis: sell-through → sell-in). GAAP loss -$2.7B vs 'cash earnings' +$9.6B over 3 years. (Ch.15, Ch.17)
_key_cases_from_book.Li_and_Fung: Contingent consideration reserve reduction: $198M gain = 51% of operating profit. (Ch.15)
_key_cases_from_book.Krispy_Kreme: Sold equipment to franchise being acquired for $700K then raised acquisition price by same $700K — circular revenue. (Ch.15)
_key_cases_from_book.Olympus: Tobashi scheme: $2B siphoned to cover bad investments via inflated acquisition 'fees' (30% of deal value vs normal 1-2%). (Ch.15)

_required_data_not_in_csv: Goodwill and intangibles (to detect acquisitive companies and goodwill impairment risk)
Acquisition spending vs. total investing cash flow (Screener.in lumps capex+M&A in 'Investing Cash Flow')
Exceptional items / restructuring charges (needed for AA#3 non-GAAP vs GAAP gap)
Non-GAAP adjusted earnings vs. GAAP net income (management's own metric, not in Screener.in)
Pre-acquisition revenue of target companies (cross-company analysis required)
Contingent consideration liabilities (balance sheet footnote data)
aa_shenanigans_implementation_gap._proxy_rejected: Investing CF / Operating CF ratio was considered as a proxy for serial-acquirer AA#2 inflation, but rejected because: (1) Screener.in 'Investing Cash Flow' combines capex AND acquisitions with no breakout; (2) capital-intensive but legitimate companies (utilities, infrastructure) would generate identical false positives; (3) without goodwill growth data, cannot distinguish acquisition-driven investing from organic capex.
aa_shenanigans_implementation_gap._future_implementation_path: If Screener.in exports 'Goodwill' and 'Intangibles' balance sheet rows and 'Exceptional Items', a 5th checker (AA proxy) could be built: goodwill_growth_3y > 30% of total_assets_growth AND exceptional_items_pct_of_pat > 15% for 3 consecutive years.

missing_csv_columns_gap._title: Missing High-Signal Columns — Required to Complete Schilit Full Coverage
missing_csv_columns_gap._status: NOT IMPLEMENTED — columns absent from Screener.in CSV export
missing_csv_columns_gap._source: BUGS.TXT forensic audit + ALL_INDICATOR_AVAILABLES (STOCKSCAN).csv schema analysis
missing_csv_columns_gap._critical_finding: EBIT and EBIT 1 Year Back are present in the Screener.in Income Statement CSV but were NOT mapped in data_engine.py INCOME_COLS. This has been FIXED in v1.3. EBIT is now mapped, enabling D&A = EBITDA - EBIT and dep_rate computation (Signal 6). The 3 remaining columns below genuinely do not exist in Screener.in exports.

resolved_column.status: RESOLVED — added to INCOME_COLS in data_engine.py v1.3
resolved_column.enables: depreciation = ebitda - ebit; dep_rate = depreciation / fixed_assets * 100; Signal 6 (EMS #4 useful-life extension)

column_1_other_income.schilit_shenanigan: EMS #3 (Boosting Income Using One-Time or Unsustainable Activities)
column_1_other_income.schilit_chapter: Ch.5 EMS#3 — Intel option income, Marvell tax engineering, Sunbeam insurance settlements
column_1_other_income.why_critical: Other Income allows exact computation of core_operating_earnings = PBT - other_income. If core operating earnings decline while total PAT expands, EMS#3 fires precisely. Current proxy (npm - opm > 5.0, Signal 4) captures the same signal approximately via margin gap, but cannot identify the absolute Rupee amount of non-operating income.
column_1_other_income.proxy_currently_implemented: Signal 4 (npm - opm > 5.0) — NPM exceeds OPM by 5+ pp detects the margin-level effect of significant Other Income. This is a good proxy but misses absolute level checks.
column_1_other_income.proposed_signal_when_available: other_income / pbt > 30.0% AND other_income > other_income_1yb * 1.50 (Other Income grew >50% YoY AND now = >30% of PBT)

column_2_intangibles_under_development.schilit_shenanigan: EMS #4 (Shifting Current Expenses to a Later Period — Capitalisation)
column_2_intangibles_under_development.schilit_chapter: Ch.6 EMS#4 — asset-light sector expense capitalisation (SaaS, Pharma R&D, E-Commerce)
column_2_intangibles_under_development.why_critical: CWIP in Indian accounting tracks tangible fixed assets (factories, machinery, land). Software companies capitalise developer payroll as 'proprietary software' and pharma companies capitalise clinical trial costs as 'product development intangibles' — neither shows in CWIP. Only Intangibles Under Development catches this. Current CWIP-based checks miss 100% of asset-light sector expense capitalisation.
column_2_intangibles_under_development.proxy_currently_implemented: Signal 5 (asset_turnover decline + OPM improvement) — catches the effect (margins improve as assets grow) but cannot distinguish capitalised tangibles from capitalised intangibles.
column_2_intangibles_under_development.proposed_signal_when_available: (intangibles_dev_current - intangibles_dev_1yb) / max(revenue, 1) > 5% AND rev_gr_yoy < 15% (intangibles growing >5% of revenue without corresponding revenue growth)

column_3_contingent_liabilities.schilit_shenanigan: KMS #6 (Distorting Balance Sheet Metrics via Off-Balance-Sheet Exposures)
column_3_contingent_liabilities.schilit_chapter: Ch.14 KMS#6, Ch.13 KMS#5 — IL&FS (347 entities, Rs 91,000 Cr hidden), Enron SPVs
column_3_contingent_liabilities.why_critical: True contingent liabilities (corporate guarantees to subsidiaries, disputed tax liabilities, pending litigation) sit entirely in report footnotes and DO NOT register under Total Liabilities until a default or adverse judgment occurs. The current hidden_obligation_growth proxy (total_liabilities - debt) cannot see these exposures. Only explicit Contingent Liabilities catches the IL&FS and Enron pattern before they materialise.
column_3_contingent_liabilities.proxy_currently_implemented: Checker 3 Signal 1 (hidden_obligation_growth = total_liabilities growing faster than debt) — catches standard off-BS obligations but blind to true contingent liabilities that don't appear in any balance sheet line until triggered.
column_3_contingent_liabilities.proposed_signal_when_available: contingent_liabilities / max(equity, 1) > 30.0% (contingent liabilities exceed 30% of equity — critical KMS#6 off-balance-sheet shield)

missing_csv_columns_gap._implementation_summary: 3 of 4 gap columns from BUGS.TXT remain unimplemented. EBIT was already in the CSV and is now mapped (RESOLVED). The 3 remaining columns (Other Income, Intangibles Under Development, Contingent Liabilities) must be added to the Screener.in stockscan export configuration. Once added, signals can be implemented within 1 session as the data_engine and forensic_engine architecture is already built to receive them.

schilit_forensic_score.interpretation: 100 = no manipulation signals. 85 = 1 checker fired. 70 = 2 checkers fired. 55 = 3 checkers. 40 = all 4.

schilit_pass.interpretation: 1 = Clean (0-2 checkers fired). 0 = Flagged (3-4 checkers fired). Used in fw_str as 'Financial Shenanigans' label.

scoring_engine.guard_import: if 'forensic_score' not in df.columns: imports and calls compute_schilit_forensic_score
